title: Allow decompressing while copying
body:
:wave: skopeo maintainers! First (micro)feature PR for me - please bear with me, I might need your guidance!


Idea is to teach `skopeo copy` to `--dest-decompress` layers (dually to the already-present `--dest-compress` option).

Use case is for `rsync`/`bsync`-based delta transfer of layers to save bandwidth, which benefits from being decompressed.

I noticed `container/image` already has all code to make this happen (and rewriting descriptors on the fly) so this PR is just about exposing that functionality via a new commandline option.


This PR has the `DO NOT MERGE` flag until the accompanying `containers/image` PR (https://github.com/containers/image/pull/1294) is merged.

Feedback more than welcome!

comments.body: the `--dest-decompress` option doesn't seem to be documented in manpages yet

> Go code looks good, please update also `docs/skopeo-copy.1.md` and `completions/bash/skopeo` .

Done
@mtrmac @vrothberg is the `/dev/tcp/127.0.0.1/5000: No route to host` a flake? It failed during a rerun, so maybe a constant failure.

> @mtrmac @vrothberg is the `/dev/tcp/127.0.0.1/5000: No route to host` a flake? It failed during a rerun, so maybe a constant failure.

Yes, I think so. I've seen it a number of times.
weird, now it shows all green.
sorry, I was reading it wrong. The re-run passed: https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5910583758290944
@moio perhaps we need a rebase, unable to hit merge otherwise.
